title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas

seo.title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) - Easy & Authentic Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) with this easy recipe. Warm, hearty, and packed with flavor – perfect for any meal!

h2Headings: What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas)?
Ingredients for Lebanese Red Lentil Soup
How to Make Lebanese Red Lentil Soup: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Best Adas Soup
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of warm, authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) garnished with fresh herbs and a lemon wedge.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of homemade Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, a staple of Middle Eastern cuisine.

description: This comforting Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known as Adas, is a cherished staple in Middle Eastern homes, offering a blend of warmth and nourishment. Made with simple, wholesome ingredients, it boasts a rich, earthy flavor brightened by a hint of lemon. Perfect for a cozy meal, this hearty soup embodies the soul of traditional Lebanese cooking.

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. Chop the onion and carrot finely, and mince the garlic. This preparation ensures all ingredients are ready for a smooth cooking process.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and carrot and sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Add Lentils and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the rinsed red lentils, ground cumin, and ground turmeric. Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to toast the spices and coat the lentils, which enhances their flavor. This step is crucial for developing the soup's aromatic base.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Pour in the vegetable broth or water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have mostly broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Blend and Season
instructions.text: Once the lentils are tender, remove the pot from the heat. For a smoother consistency, use an immersion blender to partially blend the soup, leaving some texture, or blend about half of the soup in a regular blender and return it to the pot. Stir in the fresh lemon juice, salt, and black pepper. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed.

instructions.title: Serve Adas
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) into bowls. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro or parsley. Serve immediately with a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil and a wedge of fresh lemon on the side, alongside warm pita bread if desired.

tips: For an extra layer of flavor, consider adding a bay leaf to the soup while it simmers, removing it before blending. This is a common practice in many Middle Eastern lentil soups.
Do not over-blend the soup; a slightly chunky texture is traditional and adds to the rustic charm of Adas. If you prefer a completely smooth soup, blend it all.
Adas thickens as it cools. If you have leftovers, you might need to add a splash of water or broth when reheating to achieve the desired consistency.
article:
Discover the heartwarming embrace of Adas, the beloved Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, a dish that transcends mere sustenance to become a symbol of comfort, tradition, and wholesome goodness in Middle Eastern cuisine. This simple yet profoundly flavorful soup is a staple in Lebanese households, cherished for its nutritional value, ease of preparation, and the way it brings families together around the table. Whether you're seeking a light lunch, a comforting dinner, or a nourishing start to a meal, Lebanese Red Lentil Soup offers a delightful culinary journey.

## What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas)?

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known locally as Adas (عَدَس), is a traditional, plant-based soup made primarily from red lentils, onions, garlic, and an invigorating squeeze of fresh lemon juice. Unlike some other lentil soups that might incorporate meat or a heavy base, Adas is celebrated for its purity and simplicity, allowing the earthy flavor of the lentils to shine through, brightened by the citrus and aromatic vegetables. It’s a naturally vegan and gluten-free dish that exemplifies the healthy and vibrant characteristics of Mediterranean cooking. Often enjoyed during colder months, fasting periods, or simply as a nutritious everyday meal, Adas is a testament to how humble ingredients can create something truly extraordinary and deeply satisfying. Its creamy texture, often achieved by partially or fully blending the soup, makes it incredibly appealing, even to those who might typically shy away from legumes.

## Ingredients for Lebanese Red Lentil Soup

The beauty of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup lies in its straightforward ingredient list, most of which are pantry staples. Here’s what you’ll need to create this comforting bowl of deliciousness:

*   **Red Lentils:** The star of the show. These quick-cooking legumes break down easily, creating the soup's signature creamy texture.
*   **Onion & Garlic:** The aromatic foundation. Sautéing these until fragrant builds a deep layer of flavor.
*   **Olive Oil:** Essential for sautéing and drizzling over the finished soup for richness and authentic taste. Use good quality extra virgin olive oil.
*   **Vegetable Broth or Water:** The liquid base. Broth adds more depth, but water works perfectly well, especially with the other strong flavors.
*   **Lemon Juice:** Crucial for brightening the soup and cutting through the richness of the lentils. Freshly squeezed is non-negotiable!
*   **Cumin:** The primary spice, it complements lentils beautifully, adding warmth and an earthy aroma characteristic of Middle Eastern cooking.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To season and enhance all the flavors.
*   **Optional Spices:** A pinch of turmeric can add a lovely golden color and a subtle earthy note. Coriander powder can also be used for extra depth.
*   **Fresh Mint or Cilantro:** For garnish, adding a burst of freshness.

## How to Make Lebanese Red Lentil Soup: Step-by-Step

Creating a delicious pot of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is surprisingly simple. Follow these steps for a perfect bowl every time:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat a generous drizzle of olive oil over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
3.  **Add Lentils and Spices:** Stir in the rinsed lentils and the ground cumin (and any other optional spices like turmeric or coriander). Sauté for a minute, stirring constantly, to toast the spices and coat the lentils. This enhances their flavor.
4.  **Pour in Liquid:** Add the vegetable broth or water to the pot. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the lentils are completely tender and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
5.  **Blend (Optional):** For a smoother, creamier soup, use an immersion blender directly in the pot to blend a portion or all of the soup until it reaches your desired consistency. Alternatively, carefully transfer batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot. Be cautious when blending hot liquids.
6.  **Season and Finish:** Stir in the fresh lemon juice, salt, and black pepper to taste. Adjust seasonings as needed. The lemon is key, so don’t be shy!
7.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup into bowls.

## Tips for the Best Adas Soup

While the recipe for Adas is straightforward, a few tips can elevate your Lebanese Red Lentil Soup from good to truly exceptional:

*   **Fresh Lemon is Key:** Do not skimp on fresh lemon juice. It's the essential bright counterpoint to the earthy lentils. Add it at the end to preserve its vibrant flavor.
*   **Don't Rush the Aromatics:** Sautéing the onion and garlic until they are deeply fragrant and translucent forms the bedrock of the soup's flavor.
*   **Taste and Adjust:** Lentils absorb salt, so season gradually and taste before adding more. The balance of salt, cumin, and lemon is crucial.
*   **Quality Olive Oil:** A final swirl of good quality extra virgin olive oil just before serving adds a luxurious finish and authentic Lebanese flavor.
*   **Toasted Spices:** Lightly toasting the cumin with the onions and lentils before adding liquid enhances its aroma and deepens its flavor profile.
*   **Consistency Control:** If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your preferred consistency. If too thin, let it simmer uncovered for a few more minutes to reduce.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways. It’s often served as a light meal on its own, especially for lunch, or as a comforting appetizer before a larger spread.

*   **With Pita Bread:** A quintessential pairing. Use warm pita bread for dipping and scooping up the delicious soup. Toasted pita croutons also add a lovely crunch.
*   **Fresh Garnishes:** A sprinkle of fresh chopped mint or cilantro is a must. A generous drizzle of extra virgin olive oil, a pinch of sumac, or a dash of Aleppo pepper (for a mild kick) adds visual appeal and extra layers of flavor.
*   **Side Salad:** Pair Adas with a simple side salad, like a traditional Fattoush or Tabbouleh, for a complete and healthy meal that celebrates Lebanese flavors.
*   **Pickles:** Lebanese pickled turnips or cucumbers offer a sharp, tangy contrast that complements the soup beautifully.

## Variations and Dietary Adaptations

One of the great things about Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is how naturally accommodating it is to various diets and how easily it can be adapted:

*   **Vegan/Vegetarian:** Adas is inherently vegan and vegetarian, relying solely on plant-based ingredients.
*   **Gluten-Free:** As it uses lentils and no wheat products, it's naturally gluten-free, making it a safe and delicious option for those with gluten sensitivities.
*   **Add Vegetables:** For extra nutrients and texture, you can add finely diced carrots, celery, or even a potato along with the lentils. They will cook down and add sweetness and body.
*   **Spicier Kick:** If you enjoy a bit of heat, add a pinch of red pepper flakes or a dash of hot sauce to the soup.
*   **Herbal Boost:** Beyond mint and cilantro, a sprig of thyme or a bay leaf added during simmering can infuse subtle additional aromas. Remove bay leaf before blending.
*   **Thicker or Thinner:** Adjust the amount of liquid to achieve your preferred consistency. For a heartier stew-like texture, use less liquid; for a lighter soup, add more.

Whether you're new to Middle Eastern cuisine or a long-time enthusiast, this vibrant and nourishing Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) is a recipe you'll want to keep in your rotation. Its simplicity, incredible flavor, and healthful properties make it a true culinary gem, ready to warm your soul and delight your taste buds. Give this traditional Lebanese staple a try and experience the comforting magic of Adas.
